claim: 40% of all annual canine HRI cases in the UK occur in July, with zero cases in February, October, or December — confirming sharp seasonal concentration in peak summer.
stance_target: Algarve lost dog searches in June–September operate under a qualitatively different heat risk regime than off-season searches
scope_conditions: 395 HRI events from 905,543 UK dogs under primary veterinary care in 2016
quoted_support: There were no HRI events during February, October or December, while 40% (158/395) of the incident cases were in July, corresponding with a heatwave event thought to have been triggered by a particularly strong El Nino.

claim: Brachycephalic dogs have 2.10x the odds of HRI vs mesocephalic; dogs >50 kg have 3.42x the odds; dogs ≥12 years have 1.75x the odds.
stance_target: breed type, weight, and age are the three strongest predictor variables for heat triage prioritisation in field rescue
scope_conditions: multivariable logistic regression, 905,543 UK dogs under vet care, 2016
quoted_support: Brachycephalic dogs had higher odds of HRI (OR 2.10, 95% CI 1.68-2.64) compared to mesocephalic dogs. Dogs over 50 kg in bodyweight had 3.42 times the odds.

claim: Overall HRI event fatality rate in UK primary care is 14.18%; 64.81% of deaths were by euthanasia rather than unassisted death, suggesting cases reach vets in time for triage.
stance_target: when owners identify HRI and take dogs to a vet, most survive (85.82%); the critical gap is owner recognition and rapid transport
quoted_support: The event fatality rate for HRI in dogs during 2016 was 14.18% (95% CI 11.08 – 17.96%). Of the remaining 54 deaths, 35 (64.81%) were by euthanasia and 19 (35.19%) were unassisted deaths.

claim: Chow Chow has the highest HRI incidence rate of any breed at 0.50%, followed by Bulldog (0.42%) and French Bulldog (0.18%); Greyhound is also high at 0.15%.
stance_target: breed-specific HRI incidence rates are actionable for triage prioritisation when a lost dog's breed is known
scope_conditions: breed-specific incidence from UK primary care VetCompass data 2016
quoted_support: Breeds with the highest incidence of HRI were the Chow Chow (0.50%, 95% CI 0.21-1.16%), Bulldog (0.42%, 95% CI 0.30-0.58%), French Bulldog (0.18%, 95% CI 0.12-0.25%), Dogue de Bordeaux (0.17%, 95% CI 0.07-0.39%), Greyhound (0.15%, 95% CI 0.07-0.29%).
